title: Stop When The Repair Changes
source_basis: Mode: Critique. The active frontier asks whether translation strain can expose false convergence rather than merely decorate comparison.

Primary-text close read: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad 3.7.23 treats the unseen seer and inner knower as the authority-bearing self, while SN 22.59 tests form, feeling, perception, formations, and consciousness as not fit for ownership or self-identification. The shared move is de-objectifying ordinary identity; the repair and permitted conclusion differ. Sources: https://sacred-texts.com/hin/sbe15/sbe15070.htm and https://suttacentral.net/sn22.59/en/bodhi

Practitioner-method source: Dao De Jing 48, learning by decreasing and non-forcing. I used it as a subtractive lens: remove borrowed agreement until the remaining repair can be seen. Its weakness is that it can over-reduce living speech and hide needed institution, vow, and relationship. Source card: daoism-dao-de-jing-chapter-48, https://ctext.org/dictionary.pl?id=11634&if=en

Practice-method contrast: MN 1 Mulapariyaya Sutta warns against conceiving experience as mine, in, from, or as a ground for identity. This corrects the Daoist subtraction method by asking where the analyst is adding hidden ownership to the comparison. Source card: early-buddhism-mn-1-mulapariyaya-sutta, https://suttacentral.net/mn1/en/bodhi
Closest prior art: Raimon Panikkar's homeomorphic equivalence, which compares concepts by functional role while respecting difference, https://www.raimon-panikkar.org/english/gloss-homeomorphic.html
Near prior art: Oliver Freiberger's comparative methodology of selection, description, juxtaposition, redescription, rectification, and theory formation, https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/9/2/38

Near prior art: Jonathan Z. Smith's comparison method, which treats comparison as scholarly redescription rather than discovery of natural sameness, https://classics.osu.edu/sites/classics.osu.edu/files/Magic_Dwells.pdf

Modern human-condition grounding: U.S. Surgeon General social connection advisory for loneliness and disconnection, https://www.hhs.gov/sites/default/files/surgeon-general-social-connection-advisory.pdf; Pew meaning in life report, https://www.pewresearch.org/religion/2018/11/20/where-americans-find-meaning-in-life/; Youth mental health and social media advisory for comparison and visibility pressure, https://www.hhs.gov/surgeongeneral/reports-and-publications/youth-mental-health/social-media/index.html

Empirical-adjacent safety source: Lindahl et al., Varieties of Contemplative Experience, found that similar meditation-related experiences can be interpreted, supported, and managed differently depending on practitioner, practice, relationships, health behaviors, and authority context, https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0176239

Internal prior pressure: No Word Travels Alone and First Ask What the Words Do already cover source role, authority, receiver wound, and speech role. This record narrows the active frontier to a repair-survival gate rather than a new general theory of religious language.
original_claim: A borrowed spiritual sentence is not ready to teach merely because its meaning seems to fit. It is ready only when the repair it asks for survives the crossing: the same wounded habit is being corrected, the authority and verification are not secretly replaced, and the action it produces can be checked in ordinary life. If the old repair is dropped and a new repair is supplied, the honest result is not shared wisdom; it is a new medicine that must be tested under its own name.
why_it_might_be_new: The closest prior argument is homeomorphic equivalence: concepts may play similar roles in different systems without being identical. Comparative-method work already teaches redescription, difference, and caution about invented sameness. The exact difference here is narrower and more practical: role-similarity is not enough for doctrine-building unless the repair target also survives. This also revises local translation-strain work by adding a stopping rule: when claim unit, role, ontology, agency, practice aim, authority, verification, bends, drops, and additions show that the human repair has changed, the comparison must be downgraded from convergence to a new testable proposal.
critique: This may psychologize traditions that do not frame their speech as repair. Pure Land nembutsu, Dzogchen pointing-out, sacramental speech, and communal prayer may work as entrustment, recognition, vow, or participation rather than as a remedy for a named modern wound. The model also risks making every teaching pass through modern therapeutic usefulness, which could flatten worship, metaphysics, ritual duty, and liberation claims. It weakens sharply if trained practitioners say the repair field adds no decision value beyond ordinary source criticism, teacher guidance, or spiritual-bypass screening.
next_research_directions: Build the one-page translation-strain sheet with fields for claim unit, role, ontology, agency, practice aim, authority, verification, what is bent, what is dropped, what is added, and whether role-similarity and repair-similarity survive.

If this model is right, then comparisons such as Atman and anatta should show partial role-similarity but repair divergence when authority, verification, and final practice aim are coded. If dual-trained practitioners say the repair is the same after these fields are made explicit, the model is weakened.

If this model is right, then modern seekers who borrow selflessness, witness, surrender, or emptiness without naming the repair should show predictable misuses such as withdrawal, dissociation-like detachment, passivity, or private certainty. If misuse is unrelated to repair mismatch after controlling for teacher support and mental-health risk, the model is weakened.
Test whether the repair-survival gate changes actual scoring decisions beyond Panikkar, Freiberger, Smith, spiritual-bypass screening, and existing local word-role records.

Improve the observation method by pairing Daoist subtraction with Buddhist anti-appropriation: decrease borrowed agreement, then check whether the analyst has made the comparison serve a hidden need for certainty.

teaching_candidate.title: Borrow Only What Repairs
teaching_candidate.teaching_line: Do not borrow a teaching until you know what it is meant to heal.
teaching_candidate.doctrine_claim: A teaching that changes its repair task becomes a new teaching and needs a new test.
teaching_candidate.body:
Some words look strong because they have traveled far. Self, no-self, surrender, emptiness, silence, and witness can sound alike after enough translation. That likeness is not yet wisdom.

Ask what trouble the teaching was meant to correct. Ask who was allowed to guide it, what counted as proof, and what kind of person it was trying to form. If those answers change, carry the sentence more slowly.

A borrowed teaching should make life more truthful, more responsible, and more connected. If it makes a person vanish from duty, harden into certainty, or turn pain into a private doctrine, the repair has changed.
teaching_candidate.target_human_problem: Meaning loss, digital comparison, loneliness, and self-worth tied to spiritual or intellectual certainty.
teaching_candidate.target_cohort: Stable reflective seekers, cross-tradition readers, meditation-app users, students, caregivers, and professionals who collect spiritual language to steady themselves but may lack community correction.
teaching_candidate.pressure_survived: Survives primary-text pressure between Upanishadic unseen-seer language and early Buddhist not-self analysis by preserving a shared de-objectifying move while refusing to call the metaphysical repair the same. Survives near-prior pressure only as a narrower repair-survival gate, not as a new comparative method in general.
teaching_candidate.falsifying_pressure: Weaken if practitioners and scholars find that the repair field does not change any comparison decision, if ordinary teacher guidance performs better, or if the practice increases loneliness, rumination, cynicism, or avoidance.

practice_candidate.title: The Repair Check
practice_candidate.practice_line: Before using a borrowed spiritual sentence, name the wound, the guardrail, and one ordinary action.
practice_candidate.purpose: To test whether a spiritual idea is helping the person repair a real habit or merely giving a borrowed language for private certainty.
practice_candidate.target_human_problem: Meaning loss, digital comparison, loneliness, and achievement-contingent self-worth in stable people who turn spiritual language into identity proof.
practice_candidate.target_cohort: Stable adults or older teens with ordinary reflective capacity, some social support, and no current crisis, who are reading across traditions or using contemplative apps without a teacher.
practice_candidate.non_fit: Not for acute grief, panic, psychosis, mania, dissociation, active addiction withdrawal, medical blackout concerns, OCD or scrupulosity loops, unsafe authority situations, severe depression, or people who already have competent teacher, clinical, or community guidance for the issue.
practice_candidate.frequency: Once when a borrowed sentence feels especially compelling, not daily by default.
practice_candidate.minimum_attempt: Try it with three sentences across two weeks before judging usefulness.
steps: Write the sentence you want to use in your life.
Name the wound it seems to address: loneliness, comparison, fear, pride, shame, compulsive striving, withdrawal, or another plain problem.
Name the original carrier as far as you know it: practice role, authority, verification, and what conduct it was meant to form.
Name what changes when you use it: what is bent, what is dropped, what is added, and whether the repair still matches.

Choose one ordinary action for the next day that would show the sentence is repairing rather than excusing: make contact, finish a duty, tell the truth, rest without display, apologize, ask for help, or stop a harmful loop.
notice: Whether the sentence leads to contact or withdrawal.
Whether it lowers compulsive self-proof or gives you a more spiritual form of it.
Whether you become more accountable in ordinary life.
Whether the practice produces shame, checking, numbness, or superiority.
practice_candidate.caution: Stop if the check increases rumination, panic, shame, derealization, compulsive confession, self-punishment, or isolation. Use ordinary human help, clinical care, or trusted community support when needed.
practice_candidate.weakens_if: The check performs no better than ordinary journaling, source checking, conversation with a trusted person, or an action plan, or if it makes people more isolated and self-monitoring.

tests.title: Prior Art Repair Gate Test
tests.prediction: If the contribution is more than renamed prior art, then Panikkar, Freiberger, Smith, spiritual-bypass literature, VCE, and local word-role records will not already require repair-survival as a scoring gate for doctrine candidates.
tests.result: Pending. Initial search shows strong overlap on role, redescription, context, appraisal, and misuse, but not an exact repair-survival stopping rule tied to doctrine promotion.
tests.next_action: Close-read the strongest prior texts and downgrade novelty if repair-survival is already explicit.

tests.title: Atman Anatta Repair Falsification
tests.prediction: If the model is right, then Atman and anatta comparisons will retain a shared de-objectifying role but diverge on repair, authority, verification, and final claim. If expert coders judge the repair to be the same after these fields are explicit, the model weakens.
tests.next_action: Create a blind coding sheet for Brihadaranyaka 3.7.23, Chandogya 6.8.7, SN 22.59, and MN 1 with separate fields for claim unit, role, ontology, agency, aim, authority, verification, bends, drops, additions, role survival, and repair survival.

tests.title: Borrowed Sentence Practice Pilot
tests.prediction: Stable cross-tradition readers using The Repair Check will report fewer false all-paths-agree conclusions, fewer avoidance rationalizations, and more concrete accountable action than ordinary journaling. If ordinary journaling performs as well or better, or if loneliness and rumination rise, the practice weakens.
tests.next_action: Run a two-week non-clinical pilot with exclusions, pre/post measures for loneliness, rumination, certainty, avoidance, and concrete repair action.

tests.title: Comparative Law Repair Prediction
tests.prediction: If repair-survival matters beyond religion, then legal transplants with similar terms but changed remedy, authority, procedure, or verification should predict implementation disputes better than term-similarity alone.
tests.next_action: Compare cases such as trust, equity, due process, and rights across legal systems using both term-similarity and repair-survival coding.

tests.title: Safety Boundary Review
tests.prediction: If the practice is low risk only for the named cohort, then clinicians, teachers, or spiritual directors will endorse the exclusions and add concrete stop conditions. If they identify common harms even in stable users, the risk level should move to medium or the practice should be retired.
tests.next_action: Request review from meditation teachers, chaplains, and clinicians familiar with spiritual bypassing and meditation-related difficulties.
